relations.subject: Sinaloa martin
relations.evidence: The Sinaloa martin (Progne sinaloae) is a Vulnerable species of bird in the family Hirundinidae, the swallows and martins. It breeds in Mexico; its wintering range is not known but is believed to be in South America

relations.subject: Sinaloa martin
relations.evidence: https://www.museum.lsu.edu/~Remsen/SACCCountryLists.htm retrieved November 30, 2025 In Mexico the Sinaloa martin primarily inhabits pine and pine-oak forest in the subtropical and lower tropical zones

relations.subject: Sinaloa martin
relations.evidence: "The causes of the decline in this species are unknown." Some authors suggest that logging of Mexico's montane forest has contributed but hesitate to name that the only cause
